market_note: Stablecoin market cap hit a record $322B in June (+29% YTD), while H1 2026 hack losses fell below $1B despite a record number of incidents.

stablecoin_items.headline: Open USD (OUSD) launches with 140+ partners
stablecoin_items.summary: Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, Coinbase and BlackRock backed the June 30 launch of a zero-fee-mint stablecoin; news of the launch sent Circle's stock down 16%.

stablecoin_items.headline: USDC volume overtakes Tether
stablecoin_items.summary: Circle's USDC hit record trading volume of $1.79T, pulling ahead of Tether for the first time in this metric as competition in stablecoin rails intensifies.

stablecoin_items.headline: MiCA deadline triggers largest EU delisting wave
stablecoin_items.summary: The EU's MiCA transition period ended July 1; Coinbase, Kraken and Crypto.com restricted or delisted USDT for EU users, the biggest European stablecoin delisting event on record.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline nears
stablecoin_items.summary: Six U.S. federal agencies have until July 18 to publish the finalized regulatory framework for permitted payment stablecoin issuers.

hack_items.headline: BONK DAO governance hijack drains $20M
hack_items.summary: On July 6 an attacker bought voting power and pushed through malicious proposal BIP #76, draining ~5% of BONK's total token supply from the community treasury.

hack_items.headline: KelpDAO exploit among H1's biggest at $294M
hack_items.summary: The KelpDAO breach ranks as one of the two largest crypto hacks of H1 2026, together with the Drift Protocol hack accounting for nearly 59% of all funds stolen this year.

hack_items.headline: Same-block backrun extraction costs trader $2M
hack_items.summary: GoPlus Security flagged a same-block price-discrepancy exploit where a trader swapping 1,126 ETH received a fraction of the expected token value.

hack_items.headline: Crypto hack count hits record high in H1 2026
hack_items.summary: Incident frequency reached a record in H1 2026, though total losses fell sharply below $1B, suggesting smaller average payouts per attack.
